name: Nanobot
overview: Excellent for surgical deployments. These micro-gateways are ideal for developers looking to embed basic agentic capabilities into a specific Python script or single-purpose container, but they lack the broader ecosystem of OpenClaw.
details: Nanobot is an ultra-lightweight, Python-based AI agent framework designed as an extreme alternative to OpenClaw. Clocking in at just under 4,000 lines of code, it strips away 99% of the bulk associated with larger orchestration platforms. It focuses purely on the essential execution loop: receiving a prompt, routing it to an LLM, and triggering a predefined Python function.

The gateway sacrifices complex features like native vector databases, multi-channel gateways, and web-based GUIs in favor of absolute transparency and ease of modification. Developers can easily read the entire routing logic and modify the core behavior without navigating a sprawling directory structure. It relies on standard library tools wherever possible to minimize external dependencies.

Nanobot is best suited for developers who need to embed a simple, autonomous agent into an existing Python application or script. It is not designed to be a standalone, persistent personal assistant, but rather a surgical tool for adding targeted agentic capabilities to lightweight projects where OpenClaw would be massive overkill.
